header.description: Single scalar value: the snapshot timestamp from the selected row of neuract__LT_TABLE, formatted as dd-mm-yyyy HH:MM:SS for display in the 'Snapshot Time' header line.

rows.description: One row per tank, produced by melting the wide neuract__LT_TABLE. Each row contains the tank name (static label), level percentage from the corresponding LT column, capacity in kL (static constant), and computed current volume in kL. Covers tanks LT_112, LT_113, LT_114, LT_116, LT_118, LT_120, LT_126, LT_127, LT_128 (9 tanks total).

totals.description: No aggregated totals are required for this snapshot report.

semantics: date_from and date_to are optional pass-throughs that constrain which timestamp rows are fetched from neuract__LT_TABLE. For a snapshot report, typically only the latest single row by timestamp is used. The MELT reshape is applied post-query on the fetched row(s). Tank names and capacity constants are static configuration values positionally aligned to the melt column order and must be verified against actual plant engineering data.
transformations: Fetch the latest row (or rows within optional date range) from neuract__LT_TABLE ordered by timestamp_utc descending.
Melt the 9 wide LT columns (LT_112, LT_113, LT_114, LT_116, LT_118, LT_120, LT_126, LT_127, LT_128) into a long-format DataFrame with one row per tank, aliased as row_level.

Attach static tank name labels ('Raw Water Tank (LT_112)', 'Settling Tank (LT_113)', 'Filtration Tank (LT_114)', 'Clear Water Tank (LT_116)', 'Overhead Tank (LT_118)', 'Sump Tank (LT_120)', 'Storage Tank (LT_126)', 'Storage Tank (LT_127)', 'Storage Tank (LT_128)') as row_tank_name, positionally aligned to the melt order.
Attach static capacity constants (500, 300, 450, 200, 600, 150, 250, 100, 100 kL) as row_capacity, positionally aligned to the melt order.
Compute row_current_vol = (row_level × row_capacity) ÷ 100 using the declarative multiply then divide op.
Format timestamp_utc using format_date with pattern %d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S for the snapshot-time scalar header.
